items.title: With Hopes High for New H.I.V. Prevention Pill, Merck Takes Steps to Ensure Access
items.author: Stephanie Nolen
items.content: <article>Companies in Africa and India will make generic versions of the pill that could cost as little as $5 per person per year. But access to the drug and other new products in much of Latin America remains uncertain.</article>

items.title: Indian Universities Warn Students Against Joining Exam Protests
items.author: Akriti Sharma
items.content: <article>India’s two leading government-backed universities in Delhi have warned its students against joining the ongoing youth protests, prompting a backlash from political parties and alumni as demonstrations gather pace across the country.</article>

items.title: Europe’s Summer Heat Waves Are Getting More Deadly
items.author: Laura Millan
items.content: <article>Soaring temperatures have shocked people across the continent, with sweltering conditions made worse by climate change.</article>

items.title: China Hits Back at EU With Export Curbs on Rheinmetall, Others
items.author: Bloomberg News
items.content: <article>China slapped export controls on 14 European companies including German defense giant Rheinmetall AG, saying it acted in retaliation against the European Union’s sanctions on mainland and Hong Kong entities.</article>

items.title: Turkish Lawmaker Exodus Creates New Opposition Behind Ozgur Ozel
items.author: Firat Kozok and Taylan Bilgic
items.content: <article>Turkish opposition leader Ozgur Ozel and 90 other lawmakers resigned from the Republican People’s Party, or CHP, on Friday in order to create a new political party to mobilize a fractured opposition.</article>

items.title: American Dream Fading for Global Students Including Indian, Chinese: Live Q&A
items.author: Karoline Kan, Bhuma Shrivastava, Phoebe Sedgman and Vignesh Radhakrishnan
items.content: Bloomberg reporters take your questions on how and why the American dream is fading for students globally on Tuesday, July 28, 2026 at 2 p.m. HKT.

items.title: China Targets Offshore Trusts in Sweeping Tax Clampdown
items.author: Venus Feng
items.content: <article>China will officially begin taxing offshore trusts established by its citizens, closing a longstanding loophole used by wealthy families for asset protection and succession planning.</article>

items.title: The Artist Matthew Taylor Is Helping Trump Reshape Washington
items.author: Zachary Small
items.content: <article>Matthew Taylor, a conceptual artist and political strategist, made influential ads and documentaries produced by Steve Bannon. Now he’s assisting the White House.</article>

items.title: South African High Court Halts Ramaphosa Impeachment Hearing
items.author: S'thembile Cele
items.content: <article>A South African court halted a parliamentary inquiry into whether President Cyril Ramaphosa should be impeached over his handling of a robbery at his wildlife ranch.</article>

items.title: Trump Weighs Wider Iranian Attacks With New Truce Still Elusive
items.author: Courtney Subramanian and Eltaf Najafizada
items.content: <article>President Donald Trump said he’s considering a “massive attack” on Iran to push the nation to negotiate a peace deal, raising the prospect of even higher energy prices that have already rattled economies.</article>

items.title: China Seeks US Clarity on AI Talks as Sanction Threats Loom
items.author: Bloomberg News
items.content: <article>Chinese officials are seeking clarity from Washington on what the US expects from upcoming artificial intelligence talks, according to people familiar with the matter, as the Trump administration escalates pressure on China’s fast-rising AI sector.</article>

items.title: Bangladesh President Shahabuddin to Resign Citing Health Reason
items.author: Arun Devnath
items.content: <article>Bangladesh’s President Mohammed Shahabuddin, a long time ally of ousted Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina, will be resigning on health grounds later on Friday, according to his spokesman.</article>

items.title: Kast Replaces Chile Finance Official Juan Pablo Rodríguez on Positive Drug Test
items.author: Crayton Harrison
items.content: <article>Chile President José Antonio Kast accepted the resignation of Deputy Finance Minister Juan Pablo Rodríguez after a routine drug test returned an initial positive result.</article>

items.title: Three Clashes in a Week Escalate China-Philippines Sea Feud
items.author: Bloomberg News
items.content: <article>The Philippines and China reported their third clash in the South China Sea this week that further heightened tensions, as the US flexed its presence in disputed waters.</article>

items.title: Australia to Spend $3.2 Billion to Build Up Aukus Shipyard
items.author: James Mayger
items.content: <article>Australia intends to plow more funding into a government-run shipyard in preparation for building nuclear-powered submarines, as the nation moves to upgrade its navy and expand domestic shipbuilding capacity.</article>

items.title: FDA Panel Supports Lifting Restrictions on Four Peptides
items.author: Christina Jewett and Dana G. Smith
items.content: <article>In an all-day hearing, the agency’s scientists argued against allowing broad access to unproven drugs that the agency had banned three years ago.</article>

items.title: CJP Student Protest Tests Modi's Rising India Vision as Youth Seek Jobs
items.author: Dan Strumpf, Vignesh Radhakrishnan, Atul Dev and Aryan Gupta
items.content: The economy is failing to absorb a surging number of young unemployed graduates.

items.title: Cambodia’s Leader Pledges to Tackle Evolving Scam Networks
items.author: Philip J. Heijmans
items.content: <article>Cambodian Prime Minister Hun Manet pledged to step up a crackdown on transnational online scam networks, but warned the criminal syndicates are adapting by fracturing into smaller, decentralized cells across the region.</article>

items.title: Kagan Says Supreme Court Has Stood Up to Trump in Key Cases
items.author: Abbie VanSickle
items.content: <article>At an annual meeting of judges and lawyers, the liberal justice defended her conservative colleagues from criticism that they have rubber-stamped President Trump’s agenda.</article>

items.title: Democratic Voters Keep Defying the Party Establishment
items.author: Katie Glueck
items.content: <article>The House Democratic campaign arm has stepped up its interventions in high-profile contested primaries. But this week, it suffered its third primary defeat in two months.</article>

items.title: Trump and Thune Clash Over SAVE America Act
items.author: Michael Gold
items.content: <article>A White House spokeswoman said President Trump’s patience was running thin. The top Senate Republican said she should try finding the votes.</article>

items.title: Trump Rebuilds Tariffs With New Levies on 60 Economies
items.author: Alicia Diaz and Laura Curtis
items.content: <article>The US will collect duties of between 10% and 12.5% on imports from most major trading partners, its biggest move yet to reconstruct President Donald Trump’s tariff wall that was struck down by the Supreme Court.</article>

items.title: Clint Reilly, Political Consultant Known for Feuding, Dies at 79
items.author: Trip Gabriel
items.content: <article>As savvy as he could be volatile, he advised Nancy Pelosi and Dianne Feinstein, ran for mayor of San Francisco and bought The San Francisco Examiner.</article>

items.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
items.author: Sheryl Gay Stolberg
items.content: <article>Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.</article>

items.title: South African Deputy Minister Quits $220 Billion-Fund Post
items.author: Ntando Thukwana and S'thembile Cele
items.content: <article>South Africa’s deputy finance minister quit as the chairman of a state-owned firm that oversees $220 billion of government pensions, days before he faced being removed from the post.</article>

items.title: House Rebukes Trump on Iran in War Powers Vote as Senate Blocks Debate
items.author: Robert Jimison
items.content: <article>For the second time, a few House Republicans joined Democrats to back a measure calling on the president to seek authorization for the war.</article>

items.title: Israel Welcomes Trump’s Demand for Diplomatic Ties With Saudi Arabia
items.author: David M. Halbfinger
items.content: <article>Israeli officials embraced the possibility of normalized ties, but analysts said the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal nonetheless remained bad for Israel.</article>

items.title: As Part of Nuclear Deal, Trump Demands Saudi Arabia Join the Abraham Accords
items.author: David E. Sanger and Karoun Demirjian
items.content: <article>The president’s new condition could create a roadblock to the agreement his administration announced this week.</article>

items.title: Secret Service Agent in JD Vance’s Detail Under Investigation
items.author: Karoun Demirjian
items.content: <article>The Secret Service said that a member of Vice President JD Vance’s team was accused of “compromising operational and information security.”</article>

items.title: Ukraine’s Fedorov Snubs Zelenskyy Job Offers, Seeking Return
items.author: Volodymyr Verbianyi and Aliaksandr Kudrytski
items.content: <article>Ukraine’s popular former Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov rejected all jobs offered to him by President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, saying he would only accept reinstatement to his old position, a move that could breathe new fire into nationwide protests backing him.</article>

items.title: Supreme Court Turbocharges Presidential Power
items.author: Adam Liptak
items.content: <article>A pair of decisions reshaped the structure of the federal government.</article>

items.title: Trump Threatens to Intensify Attacks on Iran as Houthis Join War
items.author: Courtney Subramanian and Eltaf Najafizada
items.content: <article>President Donald Trump threatened to escalate strikes on Iran and said he’d hold the country responsible for any further attacks by the Yemen-based Houthis on ships in the Red Sea, as the widening Middle East war puts new strain on global energy supplies.</article>

items.title: Lawmakers Worry Trump’s Team Isn’t Closing China Chip Curbs Gap They Created
items.author: Mackenzie Hawkins
items.content: <article>US lawmakers are pressing President Donald Trump’s team to close a loophole they say the administration created in China tech restrictions, but which a key agency denies exists — an example of the persistent confusion surrounding Washington’s efforts to limit China’s AI prowess.</article>

items.title: Amish Shah Wins Democratic Primary in Swing Arizona House District
items.author: Tim Balk
items.content: <article>Dr. Amish Shah, who beat the choice of the party’s House campaign arm, will face Jay Feely, a Republican, in a battleground district that covers parts of Phoenix.</article>
